name: Sierra de Córdoba – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cordoue offers diverse landscapes, from the rugged hills and holm oak forests of the Sierra Morena to the olive groves and river valleys of the Sierras Subbéticas. The region features a network of trails, including old railway paths with viaducts and tunnels, as well as dense forests and waterfalls in the Sierra de Córdoba. This varied terrain provides opportunities for mountain bikers of all skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Cordoue The most popular mountain bike trail is Guadalmellato Canal and La Campiñuela Loop, a 15.7 miles (25.3 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Guadalmellato Canal, offering scenic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Córdoba – ruta circular, a difficult 31.7 miles (51.0 km) path. This challenging route ascends through the Sierra de Córdoba, providing significant elevation gain and panoramic vistas. Local mountain bikers also love the Puente de Hierro, Cueva de la Trinidad, Ruinas de Orive, Mirador de San José – ruta circular, a 10.7 miles (17.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with historical points of interest, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Mountain biking around Cordoue is defined by Sierra Morena hills, vast olive groves, and the historic Vía Verde del Aceite. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to demanding adventures. The routes in Cordoue are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 5000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cordoue's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Cordoue?
A: Cordoue offers a wide variety of mountain bike trails, with over 160 routes available on komoot. These range from easy rides to challenging adventures, catering to all skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cordoue?
A: The most pleasant seasons for mountain biking in Cordoue are autumn and spring. During these times, temperatures are milder, making for more comfortable rides. Summer months can be very hot and are generally less recommended for strenuous activity.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cordoue?
A: You'll encounter diverse terrain, from the rugged hills, holm oak forests, and pastures of the Sierra Morena to the vast olive groves and river valleys. The Sierras Subbéticas feature old railway paths with viaducts and tunnels, while the Sierra de Córdoba offers dense forests and mountain peaks.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Cordoue?
A: Yes, Cordoue has plenty of options for less experienced riders and families. About 32 of the routes are classified as easy. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987465354/guadalmellato-canal-and-la-campinuela-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalmellato Canal and La Campiñuela Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the canal and offers scenic views on an easy path.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Cordoue?
A: Absolutely. For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Cordoue offers over 50 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987477661/sierra-de-cordoba-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Córdoba – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 51 km path with significant elevation gain and panoramic vistas, popular among local riders.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cordoue?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cordoue are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Riders often praise the varied landscapes, well-maintained paths, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Cordoue?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Cordoue are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987699923/puente-de-hierro-cueva-de-la-trinidad-ruinas-de-orive-mirador-de-san-jose-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Hierro, Cueva de la Trinidad, Ruinas de Orive, Mirador de San José – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied terrain with historical points of interest.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with waterfalls or other natural features?
A: Yes, the region boasts several natural attractions. You can find trails that lead to natural springs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4631343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elephant Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, or explore areas with waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/624215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Popea Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra de Córdoba also features dense forests and river valleys.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Cordoue?
A: Cordoue's trails often pass by significant historical sites. You might encounter ruins, ancient bridges, or even ride near impressive structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4041503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mosque–Cathedral of Córdoba&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3176628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Christian Monarchs&lt;/a&gt;. The Vía Verde del Aceite, for instance, traverses historic towns and old railway infrastructure.

Q: Are there options for e-biking in Cordoue?
A: While specific e-bike routes aren't highlighted, the diverse network of mountain bike trails in Cordoue, including both easy greenways and more challenging ascents, makes it a suitable region for e-biking. E-bikes can help riders of varying fitness levels enjoy the longer or more strenuous routes.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that explore the Sierra Morena region?
A: Definitely. The Sierra Morena, located north of Cordoue, is a prime area for mountain biking, known for its rugged terrain, holm oak forests, and historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987793435/sierra-morena-gr-48-and-elephant-fountain-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena: GR 48 and Elephant Fountain Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that allows you to experience this distinctive landscape.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in Cordoue?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the Subbética Greenway (Vía Verde del Aceite) in the Sierras Subbéticas is an excellent choice. This old railway path offers a scenic, long-distance route through olive groves, viaducts, and tunnels, perfect for a marathon-type MTB experience.
tips: Beautiful path that connects the Gualdamellato Canal with the Lagar de Don Sancho and from there go to La Jarosa or to Los Almendros restaurant. The descent is fast and beautiful.
Livestock area. Crossroads through which you can go to the Castañar de Valdejetas, La Porrada, La Canchuela or to Santa María de Trassierra through Lovacas.

Beautiful route between chestnut trees. You have to cross the Cinco Ducados estate and two gates. It forms part of a circular route that starts from the town of Santa María de Trassierra and passes through Lovacas and La Jarosa.

In the heart of the Sierra Morena near Santa María de Trassiera are the "Baños de Popea", a unique natural spectacle with a series of small waterfalls. Nearby are some Moorish mills whose ruins can be visited
Don't let illegal wells kill this place
